phrasalVerbs.definition: To continue doing something or to keep moving forward.
examples: Please go on — I'm listening to you.
The show must go on, even in bad weather.
Go on ahead; I'll catch up in a minute.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To return to a place or situation you were in before.
examples: I need to go back home — I forgot my keys.
Let's go back to the beginning of the story.
She decided to go back to school after a year off.
Come in! The door is open.
What time did you come in last night?
The tide comes in every six hours.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To leave a place, or to be released or published.
examples: Come out and play with us!
When does the new film come out?
The sun came out after the rain.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To rise from bed or from a sitting or lying position.
examples: I get up at 7 o'clock every morning.
Get up! You're going to be late!
She got up from her chair to answer the door.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To leave a bus, train, or other vehicle; also to stop touching something.
examples: We need to get off at the next stop.
What time do you get off work?

fillInExercises.sentence: Please ___ — the meeting hasn't finished yet.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Go on' means to continue. The meeting should keep going.

fillInExercises.sentence: She left her phone at home. She needs to ___ back.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Go back' means to return to a place you were before.
'Come in' means to enter a place.

fillInExercises.sentence: The sun finally ___ out after three days of rain.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Come out' (past: came out) means to appear or emerge.

fillInExercises.sentence: It's 8:30! You need to ___ or you'll miss the bus.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Get up' means to rise from bed.

fillInExercises.sentence: We need to ___ off at the next station — our stop is next.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Get off' means to exit a vehicle.

fillInExercises.sentence: I forgot my bag. I have to ___ and get it.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Go back' means to return to a previous location.

fillInExercises.sentence: When does that new video game ___ out?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Tom, [get up]! The bus leaves in ten minutes!

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: I'm coming! Do I need to [go back] for my jacket?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: No time! Just [come out] of your room now.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: OK. Which stop do we [get off] at?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: The third stop. [Come in] through the side gate when you arrive — it's open.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Alright, let's [go on] — no more stopping!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: When did that new thriller [come out]? I missed it.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: It came out last Friday. We should [go back] and see it again.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Let's [come in] out of the cold first — I know a nice café nearby.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Good idea. Then we can [go on] to the bookshop afterwards.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: I'll [get up] early tomorrow so I can buy tickets in advance.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Don't forget to [get off] at the right stop on the way home!
